subject: DevOps
title: GitOps
description: A fast breakdown of GitOps for builders.

hook.text: What if Git was the single source of truth for everything — code, infra, and deployments?

buildup.text: GitOps stores desired state in a Git repo. An agent watches the repo and reconciles reality to match it.

discovery.text: Push a commit, and the cluster updates itself. No SSH, no manual kubectl commands, no drift.

twist.text: Rollback is just reverting a Git commit. The agent sees the old state and rolls the cluster back automatically.

climax.text: ArgoCD and Flux are the leading GitOps tools. Both watch repos and sync Kubernetes clusters continuously.

punchline.text: Git is the control plane. Push to deploy, revert to rollback.

quiz.question: How does rollback work in GitOps?
options: By reverting a Git commit, which the agent auto-applies
By manually deleting and recreating resources
